mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.09% as of July 2013), the persistent near-zero policy rate mechanically compresses the equity discount rate, supporting elevated valuations and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a full quarter as long as the rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS<0.25% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window while rate remains at ZLB.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals labor market tightening that raises consumer income confidence and forward earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=7.4 as of 2013-07, down from 7.6 three months prior
failure_condition: 20-day forward return positive-rate in triggered instances falls below 55% over any 3-year walk-forward window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration of RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer and housing demand expansion that jointly signals durable goods and construction spending strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month; the effect requires co-movement, since either series alone is noisy and sector-specific.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=424481 and HOUST=836 as of mid-2013
regime_hint: any regime with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST rise jointly month-over-month, 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline (p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During mid-cycle economic expansions, sustained INDPRO growth signals improving industrial capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this mechanism is regime-specific and weakens or reverses during late-cycle overheating when industrial growth triggers inflation and rate-hike fears.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with low inflation pressure
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns conditional on rising INDPRO are not significantly positive during mid-cycle regimes, or the sign of the IC flips negative in more than one out of three historical mid-cycle expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth above a threshold signals rising inflation risk that increases the probability of future monetary tightening, causing a short-term negative reaction in S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in earlier rate normalization.
regime_hint: low-rate environment sensitive to inflation surprises
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPI acceleration triggers are not significantly negative (p>0.05) across at least 60% of triggered instances in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent uptrend in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) reflects continuous labor demand expansion, which supports consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue growth, producing an always-on positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following month regardless of specific regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS trailing 3-month change and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDPC1 (real GDP) and RSAFS (retail sales) confirms that headline economic expansion is backed by underlying consumer demand strength rather than inventory or government components, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a full quarter; the interaction is required because GDP alone is low-frequency and revision-prone while RSAFS provides monthly confirmation.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=15648.7 and RSAFS=424481 as of mid-2013
regime_hint: any regime with GDP-retail sales co-acceleration
failure_condition: When both GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth and RSAFS trailing 3-month growth are positive, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly above unconditional baseline, or joint-signal Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis zero-lower-bound recovery regime, when FEDFUNDS remains near zero while HOUST shows a recovering uptrend, the combination signals that ultra-easy policy is successfully reflating the most rate-sensitive demand sector, reinforcing risk-asset appreciation over the following month; outside this regime the interaction has no predictive value.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.09 and HOUST=836 as of mid-2013
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS<0.25% and HOUST is rising, 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (p>0.05), or the effect disappears when re-tested outside the 2009-2015 ZLB sample.
